preamble: Assibilation of the voiced palatal stop [ɟ] to the voiced alveolar affricate [dz] is a phonological process documented in Romance and Slavic languages. This represents the conversion of a stop into an affricate articulation.

examples.note: Development of voiced palatals from Vulgar Latin into Italian affricates.
Assibilation of palatal stops in Czech and other West Slavic languages.
